title: nama hispida var. coulteri
intro: Nama hispida var. coulteri, also known as Coulter's mountain lilac or Coulter's plains beardtobeardtongue, is a perennial plant native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The plant has been reported to have various health benefits, such as reducing inflammation and relieving symptoms of colds and flu, as well as aiding in the treatment of respiratory issues. The plant can be prepared through herbal preparations like infusions, teas, and tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties and health benefits.
